pattern: Standing to the right, you see exactly one node per depth — the last one on each level — so the answer is the rightmost value of every level top to bottom.
hints: From the right, each level hides all but its rightmost node, so you want one value per depth.
Sweep level by level and keep only the last node you encounter on each level.
A depth-first walk that visits the right child first lets you record the first node seen at each new depth.

edge_inputs.description: empty tree is seen as nothing
deepest visible node sits on a left branch where the right side is empty
right side missing low down, so a left-subtree node becomes visible

stress.note: first element non-null so the root exists
